questions.sentence: Please ______ the document before submitting it to the manager.
questions.explanation: Pleaseの後には動詞の原形が続きます。「Please + 動詞の原形」は丁寧な依頼の表現です。

questions.sentence: The new employee was very ______ about starting her first day at work.
questions.explanation: be動詞の後に続く補語として形容詞が必要です。人の感情を表す場合は過去分詞形の形容詞「excited」を使います。

questions.sentence: The conference will be held ______ March 15th.
questions.explanation: 特定の日付の前には前置詞「on」を使います。「in」は月や年、「at」は時刻に使います。

questions.sentence: Ms. Tanaka ______ the marketing department for over ten years.
questions.explanation: 「for over ten years（10年以上）」という期間を表す表現があるため、現在完了形「has led」が適切です。

questions.sentence: The office will be closed ______ it is a national holiday.
questions.explanation: 「祝日なのでオフィスは閉まります」という因果関係を表すため、理由を示す接続詞「because」が正解です。

questions.sentence: Each department must submit ______ budget report by Friday.
questions.explanation: 「Each department」は単数扱いなので、所有格の代名詞は「its」が適切です。

questions.sentence: All employees are required to ______ the safety training session.
questions.explanation: 「安全研修セッションに出席する」という意味で、「attend（出席する）」が最も適切です。

questions.sentence: The manager spoke ______ to the team about the upcoming changes.
questions.explanation: 動詞「spoke」を修飾するには副詞が必要です。「directly（直接的に）」が正解です。

questions.sentence: The package was delivered ______ the reception desk this morning.
questions.explanation: 「deliver to ～（～に届ける）」という組み合わせで、前置詞「to」が正解です。

questions.sentence: We need to find a ______ for the broken printer as soon as possible.
questions.explanation: 冠詞「a」の後には名詞が必要です。「replacement（代替品）」が名詞形で正解です。

questions.sentence: The quarterly sales figures were ______ higher than the company had anticipated.
questions.explanation: 比較級「higher」を修飾するには副詞が必要です。「significantly（著しく）」が正解です。

questions.sentence: ______ the renovation is complete, the office will reopen on Monday.
questions.explanation: 「改修が完了したら、オフィスは月曜日に再開します」という意味で、「Once（一度～したら）」が最も適切です。

questions.sentence: The CEO praised the team for ______ outstanding performance during the product launch.
questions.explanation: 「the team」は集合名詞で、メンバー個々を指す場合は複数扱いとなり、所有格「their」が適切です。

questions.sentence: Applications must be received no later ______ April 30th to be considered.
questions.explanation: 「no later than ～（～までに）」は期限を表す定型表現です。「than」が正解です。

questions.sentence: By the time the clients arrive, we ______ the presentation materials.
questions.explanation: 「By the time ～（～するまでに）」は未来完了形と共に使われます。「will have prepared」が正解です。

questions.sentence: The company is looking for candidates with ______ in project management.
questions.explanation: 前置詞「with」の後には名詞が続きます。「experience（経験）」が名詞で正解です。

questions.sentence: The building is conveniently located ______ walking distance of the train station.
questions.explanation: 「within walking distance of ～（～から徒歩圏内に）」は定型表現です。「within」が正解です。

questions.sentence: Mr. Kim asked ______ could volunteer to work the weekend shift.
questions.explanation: 「誰が週末シフトにボランティアできるか」を尋ねているため、人を表す疑問代名詞「who」が正解です。

questions.sentence: The warranty will be ______ if the product is not used according to the instructions.
questions.explanation: 「説明書通りに使用されなかった場合、保証は無効になります」。「void（無効な）」が正解です。

questions.sentence: ______ the ongoing negotiations between the two firms, no public statements should be made.
questions.explanation: 「2社間の交渉が進行中であることを考慮して、公式声明は出すべきではない」。「In light of（～を考慮して）」が正解です。

questions.sentence: The consultant recommended that the company ______ its marketing strategy immediately.
questions.explanation: 「recommend that + 主語 + 動詞の原形」は仮定法現在の構文です。動詞は原形「revise」が正解です。

questions.sentence: The merger, ______ was announced last week, is expected to be finalized by December.
questions.explanation: コンマで区切られた非制限用法の関係代名詞節では「which」を使います。「that」は非制限用法では使えません。

questions.sentence: The new policy was implemented to ensure ______ with international trade regulations.
questions.explanation: 動詞「ensure」の目的語として名詞が必要です。「compliance（遵守）」が名詞形で正解です。

questions.sentence: Could you please send me a ______ of the meeting agenda?
questions.explanation: 「会議の議題のコピーを送っていただけますか」。「a copy of ～（～のコピー）」が最も自然です。

questions.sentence: The store is open ______ 9 A.M. to 6 P.M. on weekdays.
questions.explanation: 「from ～ to ～（～から～まで）」は時間の範囲を表す定型表現です。

questions.sentence: Employees who wish to participate in the training program should register ______.
questions.explanation: 主語「Employees」と目的語が同一人物を指すため、再帰代名詞「themselves」が正解です。

questions.sentence: The report indicates that customer satisfaction has ______ improved over the past year.
questions.explanation: 動詞「improved」を修飾するには副詞が必要です。「steadily（着実に）」が正解です。

questions.sentence: ______ the budget constraints, the research team managed to complete the project on time.
questions.explanation: 「予算の制約にもかかわらず」という逆接の意味が必要です。「Despite（～にもかかわらず）」が正解です。

questions.sentence: The seminar will provide participants with ______ information about investment strategies.
questions.explanation: 名詞「information」を修飾するには形容詞が必要です。「valuable（貴重な）」が正解です。

questions.sentence: Had the shipment arrived on time, we ______ able to meet the deadline.
questions.explanation: 「Had + 主語 + 過去分詞」は仮定法過去完了の倒置形です。帰結節は「would have been」が正解です。

questions.sentence: The company offers a wide ______ of health insurance plans to its employees.
questions.explanation: 「a wide range of ～（幅広い～）」は定型表現で、種類の多さを表します。

questions.sentence: The factory will remain operational ______ the inspection is being conducted.
questions.explanation: 「検査が行われている間」という同時進行を表すため、「while（～の間）」が正解です。

questions.sentence: The firm's revenues have grown ______ since the introduction of its online platform.
questions.explanation: 動詞「have grown」を修飾するには副詞が必要です。「exponentially（飛躍的に）」が正解です。

questions.sentence: The hotel is ______ to the convention center, making it ideal for business travelers.
questions.explanation: 「adjacent to ～（～に隣接した）」が文脈に合います。ホテルがコンベンションセンターに隣接しているという意味です。

questions.sentence: The architect presented several designs, ______ of which met the client's requirements.
questions.explanation: 「all of which」は関係代名詞節で「そのすべてが」を意味します。「every」は「of which」とは使えません。

questions.sentence: Visitors must sign ______ at the front desk before entering the building.
questions.explanation: 「sign in（受付で記帳する）」は建物に入る際の手続きを表す句動詞です。

questions.sentence: The company's annual revenue ______ by 15 percent compared to the previous year.
questions.explanation: 「compared to the previous year（前年と比較して）」は過去の事実を述べているため、過去形「increased」が正解です。

questions.sentence: The board of directors will not proceed with the acquisition ______ all legal concerns have been addressed.
questions.explanation: 「すべての法的懸念が解決されるまで買収を進めない」。「until（～するまで）」が文脈に合います。

questions.sentence: The receptionist asked the visitor to take a seat and wait ______ a moment.
questions.explanation: 「wait for ～（～を待つ）」の前置詞は「for」です。「wait for a moment（少々お待ちください）」。

questions.sentence: The CEO's decision to restructure the company was met with considerable ______ from long-term employees.
questions.explanation: 形容詞「considerable」の後には名詞が必要です。「resistance（抵抗）」が名詞形で正解です。

questions.sentence: The product launch has been postponed ______ further notice.
questions.explanation: 「until further notice（追って通知があるまで）」は定型表現です。

questions.sentence: Not only did the company exceed its sales targets, ______ it also expanded into three new markets.
questions.explanation: 「Not only ～ but also ～（～だけでなく～も）」は相関接続詞の構文です。「but」が正解です。

questions.sentence: The marketing team is ______ for developing the advertising campaign for the new product line.
questions.explanation: 「responsible for ～（～の責任がある）」が文脈に合います。「capable of」「eligible for」は意味が異なります。

questions.sentence: The training manual ______ last updated in January of this year.
questions.explanation: 「in January of this year（今年1月に）」という過去の特定時点を示す表現があるため、過去形「was」が正解です。

questions.sentence: The proposal was rejected on the ______ that it did not adequately address environmental concerns.
questions.explanation: 「on the grounds that ～（～という理由で）」は定型表現です。「basis」の場合は「on the basis of」となります。

questions.sentence: Passengers are advised to arrive at the airport at least two hours ______ departure.
questions.explanation: 「prior to ～（～の前に）」は「before」のフォーマルな表現です。「ahead」は単独では使えず「ahead of」が必要です。

questions.sentence: The company will ______ a new branch office in Singapore next quarter.
questions.explanation: 助動詞「will」の後には動詞の原形が続きます。「open」が正解です。

questions.sentence: ______ impressive the candidate's resume may be, a thorough interview process is still necessary.
questions.explanation: 「However + 形容詞 + S + V（どんなに～であっても）」は譲歩を表す構文です。「However impressive」が正解です。

questions.sentence: The supervisor commended the staff for their ______ to completing the project ahead of schedule.
questions.explanation: 所有格「their」の後には名詞が必要です。「dedication（献身）」が名詞形で正解です。

questions.sentence: Please make sure to turn ______ all the lights before leaving the office.
questions.explanation: 「turn off（消す）」は電気やスイッチを切る句動詞です。「turn on」はつけるという意味です。

questions.passage:
To: All Staff
From: Human Resources Department
Subject: Updated Leave Policy

Dear Colleagues,

We are writing to inform you of important changes to the company's leave policy, effective May 1st. After careful (1)______ with department heads and employee representatives, the following updates have been approved.

First, the annual paid leave allowance will increase from 15 to 18 days for employees who have completed at least two years of (2)______. New employees will continue to receive 15 days.

Second, employees may now carry over up to five unused leave days to the following year. (3)______, any days beyond five will expire at the end of the fiscal year.

Third, requests for extended leave of more than five consecutive days must be submitted at least three weeks in advance. This will help managers plan (4)______ and ensure adequate staffing levels.

Please review the full updated policy document, which is available on the company intranet. If you have any questions, do not (5)______ to contact the HR department.

Best regards,
Sarah Mitchell
HR Director
questions.sentence: Question for blank (1): After careful ______ with department heads and employee representatives, the following updates have been approved.
questions.explanation: 形容詞「careful」の後には名詞が必要です。「consultation（協議）」が名詞形で正解です。

questions.passage:
To: All Staff
From: Human Resources Department
Subject: Updated Leave Policy

Dear Colleagues,

We are writing to inform you of important changes to the company's leave policy, effective May 1st. After careful (1)______ with department heads and employee representatives, the following updates have been approved.

First, the annual paid leave allowance will increase from 15 to 18 days for employees who have completed at least two years of (2)______. New employees will continue to receive 15 days.

Second, employees may now carry over up to five unused leave days to the following year. (3)______, any days beyond five will expire at the end of the fiscal year.

Third, requests for extended leave of more than five consecutive days must be submitted at least three weeks in advance. This will help managers plan (4)______ and ensure adequate staffing levels.

Please review the full updated policy document, which is available on the company intranet. If you have any questions, do not (5)______ to contact the HR department.

Best regards,
Sarah Mitchell
HR Director
questions.sentence: Question for blank (2): ...for employees who have completed at least two years of ______.
questions.explanation: 「years of service（勤続年数）」はビジネスでよく使われる定型表現です。

questions.passage:
To: All Staff
From: Human Resources Department
Subject: Updated Leave Policy

Dear Colleagues,

We are writing to inform you of important changes to the company's leave policy, effective May 1st. After careful (1)______ with department heads and employee representatives, the following updates have been approved.

First, the annual paid leave allowance will increase from 15 to 18 days for employees who have completed at least two years of (2)______. New employees will continue to receive 15 days.

Second, employees may now carry over up to five unused leave days to the following year. (3)______, any days beyond five will expire at the end of the fiscal year.

Third, requests for extended leave of more than five consecutive days must be submitted at least three weeks in advance. This will help managers plan (4)______ and ensure adequate staffing levels.

Please review the full updated policy document, which is available on the company intranet. If you have any questions, do not (5)______ to contact the HR department.

Best regards,
Sarah Mitchell
HR Director
questions.sentence: Question for blank (3): ______, any days beyond five will expire at the end of the fiscal year.
questions.explanation: 前文では「最大5日の繰越が可能」と述べ、後文では「5日を超える分は失効する」と制限を加えています。逆接の「However」が適切です。

questions.passage:
To: All Staff
From: Human Resources Department
Subject: Updated Leave Policy

Dear Colleagues,

We are writing to inform you of important changes to the company's leave policy, effective May 1st. After careful (1)______ with department heads and employee representatives, the following updates have been approved.

First, the annual paid leave allowance will increase from 15 to 18 days for employees who have completed at least two years of (2)______. New employees will continue to receive 15 days.

Second, employees may now carry over up to five unused leave days to the following year. (3)______, any days beyond five will expire at the end of the fiscal year.

Third, requests for extended leave of more than five consecutive days must be submitted at least three weeks in advance. This will help managers plan (4)______ and ensure adequate staffing levels.

Please review the full updated policy document, which is available on the company intranet. If you have any questions, do not (5)______ to contact the HR department.

Best regards,
Sarah Mitchell
HR Director
questions.sentence: Question for blank (4): This will help managers plan ______ and ensure adequate staffing levels.
questions.explanation: 動詞「plan」を修飾する副詞が必要です。「accordingly（それに応じて）」が正解です。

questions.passage:
To: All Staff
From: Human Resources Department
Subject: Updated Leave Policy

Dear Colleagues,

We are writing to inform you of important changes to the company's leave policy, effective May 1st. After careful (1)______ with department heads and employee representatives, the following updates have been approved.

First, the annual paid leave allowance will increase from 15 to 18 days for employees who have completed at least two years of (2)______. New employees will continue to receive 15 days.

Second, employees may now carry over up to five unused leave days to the following year. (3)______, any days beyond five will expire at the end of the fiscal year.

Third, requests for extended leave of more than five consecutive days must be submitted at least three weeks in advance. This will help managers plan (4)______ and ensure adequate staffing levels.

Please review the full updated policy document, which is available on the company intranet. If you have any questions, do not (5)______ to contact the HR department.

Best regards,
Sarah Mitchell
HR Director
questions.sentence: Question for blank (5): If you have any questions, do not ______ to contact the HR department.
questions.explanation: 「do not hesitate to ～（遠慮なく～してください）」はビジネスメールでよく使われる丁寧な定型表現です。

questions.passage:
NOTICE: Office Relocation

Greenfield Technologies is pleased to announce that our headquarters will be moving to a new location at 500 Harbor Drive, Suite 300, effective June 15th.

The new office space is (1)______ larger than our current facility, providing room for our growing team. It features modern meeting rooms, an employee lounge, and improved parking facilities.

During the transition period from June 10th to June 14th, some services may be (2)______ disrupted. We ask for your patience during this time. All client meetings scheduled during this period will be conducted (3)______.

Employees are requested to pack their personal belongings and label all boxes by June 8th. The moving company will handle all furniture and (4)______.

(5)______ information about the new office, including directions and public transportation options, will be distributed next week.

Thank you for your cooperation.
Facilities Management Team
questions.sentence: Question for blank (1): The new office space is ______ larger than our current facility.
questions.explanation: 比較級「larger」を修飾するには副詞が必要です。「significantly（大幅に）」が正解です。

questions.passage:
NOTICE: Office Relocation

Greenfield Technologies is pleased to announce that our headquarters will be moving to a new location at 500 Harbor Drive, Suite 300, effective June 15th.

The new office space is (1)______ larger than our current facility, providing room for our growing team. It features modern meeting rooms, an employee lounge, and improved parking facilities.

During the transition period from June 10th to June 14th, some services may be (2)______ disrupted. We ask for your patience during this time. All client meetings scheduled during this period will be conducted (3)______.

Employees are requested to pack their personal belongings and label all boxes by June 8th. The moving company will handle all furniture and (4)______.

(5)______ information about the new office, including directions and public transportation options, will be distributed next week.

Thank you for your cooperation.
Facilities Management Team
questions.sentence: Question for blank (2): ...some services may be ______ disrupted.
questions.explanation: 移転期間中の一時的な混乱を述べているため、「temporarily（一時的に）」が正解です。

questions.passage:
NOTICE: Office Relocation

Greenfield Technologies is pleased to announce that our headquarters will be moving to a new location at 500 Harbor Drive, Suite 300, effective June 15th.

The new office space is (1)______ larger than our current facility, providing room for our growing team. It features modern meeting rooms, an employee lounge, and improved parking facilities.

During the transition period from June 10th to June 14th, some services may be (2)______ disrupted. We ask for your patience during this time. All client meetings scheduled during this period will be conducted (3)______.

Employees are requested to pack their personal belongings and label all boxes by June 8th. The moving company will handle all furniture and (4)______.

(5)______ information about the new office, including directions and public transportation options, will be distributed next week.

Thank you for your cooperation.
Facilities Management Team
questions.sentence: Question for blank (3): All client meetings scheduled during this period will be conducted ______.
questions.explanation: オフィス移転中は対面会議ができないため、「remotely（リモートで）」が文脈に合います。

questions.passage:
NOTICE: Office Relocation

Greenfield Technologies is pleased to announce that our headquarters will be moving to a new location at 500 Harbor Drive, Suite 300, effective June 15th.

The new office space is (1)______ larger than our current facility, providing room for our growing team. It features modern meeting rooms, an employee lounge, and improved parking facilities.

During the transition period from June 10th to June 14th, some services may be (2)______ disrupted. We ask for your patience during this time. All client meetings scheduled during this period will be conducted (3)______.

Employees are requested to pack their personal belongings and label all boxes by June 8th. The moving company will handle all furniture and (4)______.

(5)______ information about the new office, including directions and public transportation options, will be distributed next week.

Thank you for your cooperation.
Facilities Management Team
questions.sentence: Question for blank (4): The moving company will handle all furniture and ______.
questions.explanation: 接続詞「and」で「furniture」と並列される名詞が必要です。「equipment（設備・備品）」が正解です。

questions.passage:
NOTICE: Office Relocation

Greenfield Technologies is pleased to announce that our headquarters will be moving to a new location at 500 Harbor Drive, Suite 300, effective June 15th.

The new office space is (1)______ larger than our current facility, providing room for our growing team. It features modern meeting rooms, an employee lounge, and improved parking facilities.

During the transition period from June 10th to June 14th, some services may be (2)______ disrupted. We ask for your patience during this time. All client meetings scheduled during this period will be conducted (3)______.

Employees are requested to pack their personal belongings and label all boxes by June 8th. The moving company will handle all furniture and (4)______.

(5)______ information about the new office, including directions and public transportation options, will be distributed next week.

Thank you for your cooperation.
Facilities Management Team
questions.sentence: Question for blank (5): ______ information about the new office will be distributed next week.
questions.explanation: 名詞「information」を修飾するには形容詞が必要です。「Additional（追加の）」が正解です。

questions.passage:
Dear Mr. Nakamura,

Thank you for your recent inquiry regarding our corporate event planning services. We would be delighted to assist you with your company's annual gala dinner.

Based on the details you provided, I have prepared a preliminary proposal for your (1)______. The event would be held at the Grand Ballroom of the Riverside Hotel, which can (2)______ up to 300 guests.

Our standard corporate package includes a three-course dinner, a dedicated event coordinator, audiovisual equipment, and floral arrangements. (3)______ services such as live entertainment, photography, and custom decorations are available at an additional cost.

I would like to schedule a meeting to discuss your specific requirements in greater (4)______. Please let me know your availability during the week of March 10th.

I look forward to the (5)______ of working with you on this event.

Warm regards,
Elena Vasquez
Senior Event Planner
questions.sentence: Question for blank (1): I have prepared a preliminary proposal for your ______.
questions.explanation: 「for your review（ご確認用に）」はビジネスメールの定型表現です。所有格「your」の後に名詞「review」が続きます。

questions.passage:
Dear Mr. Nakamura,

Thank you for your recent inquiry regarding our corporate event planning services. We would be delighted to assist you with your company's annual gala dinner.

Based on the details you provided, I have prepared a preliminary proposal for your (1)______. The event would be held at the Grand Ballroom of the Riverside Hotel, which can (2)______ up to 300 guests.

Our standard corporate package includes a three-course dinner, a dedicated event coordinator, audiovisual equipment, and floral arrangements. (3)______ services such as live entertainment, photography, and custom decorations are available at an additional cost.

I would like to schedule a meeting to discuss your specific requirements in greater (4)______. Please let me know your availability during the week of March 10th.

I look forward to the (5)______ of working with you on this event.

Warm regards,
Elena Vasquez
Senior Event Planner
questions.sentence: Question for blank (2): ...which can ______ up to 300 guests.
questions.explanation: 「accommodate（収容する）」が正解です。会場が最大300名を収容できるという意味です。

questions.passage:
Dear Mr. Nakamura,

Thank you for your recent inquiry regarding our corporate event planning services. We would be delighted to assist you with your company's annual gala dinner.

Based on the details you provided, I have prepared a preliminary proposal for your (1)______. The event would be held at the Grand Ballroom of the Riverside Hotel, which can (2)______ up to 300 guests.

Our standard corporate package includes a three-course dinner, a dedicated event coordinator, audiovisual equipment, and floral arrangements. (3)______ services such as live entertainment, photography, and custom decorations are available at an additional cost.

I would like to schedule a meeting to discuss your specific requirements in greater (4)______. Please let me know your availability during the week of March 10th.

I look forward to the (5)______ of working with you on this event.

Warm regards,
Elena Vasquez
Senior Event Planner
questions.sentence: Question for blank (3): ______ services such as live entertainment, photography, and custom decorations are available at an additional cost.
questions.explanation: 名詞「services」を修飾する形容詞が必要です。「Optional（オプションの）」が正解です。

questions.passage:
Dear Mr. Nakamura,

Thank you for your recent inquiry regarding our corporate event planning services. We would be delighted to assist you with your company's annual gala dinner.

Based on the details you provided, I have prepared a preliminary proposal for your (1)______. The event would be held at the Grand Ballroom of the Riverside Hotel, which can (2)______ up to 300 guests.

Our standard corporate package includes a three-course dinner, a dedicated event coordinator, audiovisual equipment, and floral arrangements. (3)______ services such as live entertainment, photography, and custom decorations are available at an additional cost.

I would like to schedule a meeting to discuss your specific requirements in greater (4)______. Please let me know your availability during the week of March 10th.

I look forward to the (5)______ of working with you on this event.

Warm regards,
Elena Vasquez
Senior Event Planner
questions.sentence: Question for blank (4): ...to discuss your specific requirements in greater ______.
questions.explanation: 「in greater detail（より詳しく）」は定型表現です。不可算名詞として「detail」が正解です。

questions.passage:
Dear Mr. Nakamura,

Thank you for your recent inquiry regarding our corporate event planning services. We would be delighted to assist you with your company's annual gala dinner.

Based on the details you provided, I have prepared a preliminary proposal for your (1)______. The event would be held at the Grand Ballroom of the Riverside Hotel, which can (2)______ up to 300 guests.

Our standard corporate package includes a three-course dinner, a dedicated event coordinator, audiovisual equipment, and floral arrangements. (3)______ services such as live entertainment, photography, and custom decorations are available at an additional cost.

I would like to schedule a meeting to discuss your specific requirements in greater (4)______. Please let me know your availability during the week of March 10th.

I look forward to the (5)______ of working with you on this event.

Warm regards,
Elena Vasquez
Senior Event Planner
questions.sentence: Question for blank (5): I look forward to the ______ of working with you on this event.
questions.explanation: 「the opportunity of ～ing（～する機会）」が最も適切です。ビジネスメールで協力の機会を楽しみにしているという表現です。

questions.passage:
MEMO
To: Regional Sales Managers
From: VP of Sales, David Chen
Date: September 3
Re: Q4 Sales Strategy

As we approach the final quarter of the fiscal year, I want to outline our (1)______ for achieving our annual revenue goals.

Currently, we are 12% below our projected targets. (2)______, I am confident that with focused effort, we can close the gap. The holiday season traditionally accounts for 35% of our annual sales, and this year we have several advantages.

First, our new product line has received (3)______ positive reviews from industry analysts. Second, our expanded online platform will allow us to reach customers more effectively than in previous years.

I am asking each regional manager to submit a detailed action plan by September 15th. The plan should include specific targets, (4)______ strategies, and resource requirements.

A mandatory strategy meeting will be held on September 20th to (5)______ all regional plans and finalize our Q4 approach.

Let's finish the year strong.
questions.sentence: Question for blank (1): I want to outline our ______ for achieving our annual revenue goals.
questions.explanation: 所有格「our」の後には名詞が必要です。「strategy（戦略）」が名詞形で正解です。

questions.passage:
MEMO
To: Regional Sales Managers
From: VP of Sales, David Chen
Date: September 3
Re: Q4 Sales Strategy

As we approach the final quarter of the fiscal year, I want to outline our (1)______ for achieving our annual revenue goals.

Currently, we are 12% below our projected targets. (2)______, I am confident that with focused effort, we can close the gap. The holiday season traditionally accounts for 35% of our annual sales, and this year we have several advantages.

First, our new product line has received (3)______ positive reviews from industry analysts. Second, our expanded online platform will allow us to reach customers more effectively than in previous years.

I am asking each regional manager to submit a detailed action plan by September 15th. The plan should include specific targets, (4)______ strategies, and resource requirements.

A mandatory strategy meeting will be held on September 20th to (5)______ all regional plans and finalize our Q4 approach.

Let's finish the year strong.
questions.sentence: Question for blank (2): Currently, we are 12% below our projected targets. ______, I am confident that with focused effort, we can close the gap.
questions.explanation: 前文では目標を下回っていると述べ、後文では自信を示しています。逆接の「Nevertheless（それにもかかわらず）」が適切です。

questions.passage:
MEMO
To: Regional Sales Managers
From: VP of Sales, David Chen
Date: September 3
Re: Q4 Sales Strategy

As we approach the final quarter of the fiscal year, I want to outline our (1)______ for achieving our annual revenue goals.

Let's finish the year strong.
questions.sentence: Question for blank (3): ...our new product line has received ______ positive reviews from industry analysts.
questions.explanation: 形容詞「positive」を修飾するには副詞が必要です。「overwhelmingly（圧倒的に）」が正解です。

questions.passage:
MEMO
To: Regional Sales Managers
From: VP of Sales, David Chen
Date: September 3
Re: Q4 Sales Strategy

As we approach the final quarter of the fiscal year, I want to outline our (1)______ for achieving our annual revenue goals.

questions.sentence: Question for blank (4): The plan should include specific targets, ______ strategies, and resource requirements.
questions.explanation: 名詞「strategies」を修飾する形容詞的用法として「marketing（マーケティングの）」が正解です。動名詞が形容詞的に使われています。

questions.passage:
MEMO
To: Regional Sales Managers
From: VP of Sales, David Chen
Date: September 3

questions.sentence: Question for blank (5): A mandatory strategy meeting will be held on September 20th to ______ all regional plans.
questions.explanation: 会議で各地域の計画を「review（確認・検討する）」が最も適切です。「revise（修正する）」「reveal（明かす）」「reverse（逆転する）」は文脈に合いません。

questions.passage:
Westbrook Business Center
Office Space Available

Modern office suites available for immediate occupancy at Westbrook Business Center, located in the heart of downtown Portland.

Features:
- Suites ranging from 500 to 2,500 square feet
- High-speed internet included in all units
- 24-hour security and building access
- Underground parking available ($150/month per space)
- Shared conference room and kitchen facilities
- On-site fitness center

Lease Terms:
- Minimum 12-month lease
- Monthly rent: $18-$22 per square foot
- Security deposit: Two months' rent
- Utilities included (electricity, water, heating)

For inquiries, contact Westbrook Property Management at (503) 555-0142 or email leasing@westbrookbc.com.

Open house: Saturday, March 8th, 10 A.M. - 3 P.M.
questions.sentence: What is included in the monthly rent at Westbrook Business Center?
questions.explanation: 広告に「Utilities included (electricity, water, heating)」と記載されています。光熱費は家賃に含まれています。駐車場は別途月額$150です。

questions.passage:
From: Julia Sanders <j.sanders@brightoncorp.com>
To: Mark Thompson <m.thompson@brightoncorp.com>
Date: October 14
Subject: Client Presentation - Friday

Hi Mark,

I wanted to touch base about the client presentation scheduled for this Friday at 2:00 P.M. I've completed the first draft of the slide deck and would appreciate your feedback before I finalize it.

I've incorporated the latest sales data from Q3, as well as the customer satisfaction survey results that were released last week. However, I'm still waiting for the updated budget projections from the finance department. I've already followed up with Tom in finance, and he said he would have them to us by Wednesday afternoon at the latest.

Also, I think we should prepare a handout summarizing the key points for the client. Would you be able to put that together? I know you're great at condensing information into clear, concise documents.

Let me know if you'd like to meet tomorrow morning to go over the presentation together. I'm free between 9:00 and 11:00 A.M.

Thanks,
Julia
questions.sentence: What is the purpose of this email?
choices: B) To coordinate on a presentation
C) To share quarterly financial results
D) To request a budget increase
questions.explanation: このメールは金曜日のクライアントプレゼンテーションの準備について調整するためのものです。フィードバックの依頼や配布資料の作成依頼をしています。

questions.sentence: What day was this email most likely sent?

questions.passage:
Pinnacle Training Institute
Professional Development Courses - Spring Schedule

Project Management Fundamentals
Instructor: Dr. Robert Hayes
Dates: April 7 - May 12 (6 sessions, Mondays)
Time: 6:30 P.M. - 9:00 P.M.
Fee: $450 (early bird: $380 if registered before March 15)
This course covers the basics of project planning, scheduling, budgeting, and risk management. Ideal for new project managers or those seeking PMP certification preparation.

Advanced Business Communication
Instructor: Professor Lisa Chen
Dates: April 9 - May 28 (8 sessions, Wednesdays)
Time: 7:00 P.M. - 9:00 P.M.
Fee: $520 (early bird: $440 if registered before March 15)
Designed for mid-level professionals, this course focuses on persuasive writing, executive presentations, and cross-cultural communication strategies.

Financial Analysis for Non-Financial Managers
Instructor: Michael Park, CFA
Dates: April 12 - May 17 (6 sessions, Saturdays)
Time: 10:00 A.M. - 12:30 P.M.
Fee: $480 (early bird: $400 if registered before March 15)
Learn to read financial statements, analyze key performance indicators, and make data-driven business decisions.

All courses include materials and a certificate of completion.
Registration: www.pinnacletraining.com or call (212) 555-0198
Location: Pinnacle Training Center, 250 Madison Avenue, 12th Floor
questions.sentence: How much would a person save by registering early for the Advanced Business Communication course?
questions.explanation: 通常料金$520 - 早割料金$440 = $80の割引です。3月15日までに登録すると$80節約できます。

questions.passage:
Evergreen Hotels & Resorts
Guest Satisfaction Survey Results - Annual Report

Thank you for choosing Evergreen Hotels. Below is a summary of our guest feedback for the past year.

Overall Satisfaction: 4.3 out of 5.0 (up from 4.1 last year)

Category Ratings:
- Room Cleanliness: 4.6
- Staff Friendliness: 4.5
- Check-in/Check-out Speed: 3.8
- Restaurant Quality: 4.2
- Wi-Fi Reliability: 3.5
- Value for Money: 3.9

Key Findings:
- 89% of guests said they would recommend Evergreen Hotels to others
- The most common complaint was slow Wi-Fi in guest rooms
- Guests praised the newly renovated lobby and breakfast buffet
- Business travelers rated the conference facilities 4.4 out of 5.0

Action Items:
- Upgrade Wi-Fi infrastructure across all properties by Q2
- Add self-service check-in kiosks at three flagship locations
- Expand breakfast menu options based on guest suggestions
questions.sentence: What received the lowest rating from guests?
questions.explanation: Wi-Fi Reliabilityが3.5で最も低い評価です。最も一般的な苦情も「客室のWi-Fiの遅さ」でした。

questions.sentence: What action is the hotel planning to address the check-in process?
choices: A) Hiring more front desk staff
questions.explanation: Action Itemsに「Add self-service check-in kiosks at three flagship locations」と記載されています。セルフチェックイン端末の設置を計画しています。

questions.passage:
Evergreen Hotels & Resorts
Guest Satisfaction Survey Results - Annual Report

Thank you for choosing Evergreen Hotels. Below is a summary of our guest feedback for the past year.

Overall Satisfaction: 4.3 out of 5.0 (up from 4.1 last year)

Category Ratings:
- Room Cleanliness: 4.6
- Staff Friendliness: 4.5
- Check-in/Check-out Speed: 3.8
- Restaurant Quality: 4.2
- Wi-Fi Reliability: 3.5
- Value for Money: 3.9

Key Findings:
- 89% of guests said they would recommend Evergreen Hotels to others
- The most common complaint was slow Wi-Fi in guest rooms
- Guests praised the newly renovated lobby and breakfast buffet
- Business travelers rated the conference facilities 4.4 out of 5.0

Action Items:
- Upgrade Wi-Fi infrastructure across all properties by Q2
- Add self-service check-in kiosks at three flagship locations
- Expand breakfast menu options based on guest suggestions
questions.sentence: What can be inferred about the hotel's conference facilities?
choices: A) They are rarely used by guests
B) They received lower ratings than most categories
C) They are rated higher than the overall satisfaction score
D) They are only available at flagship locations
questions.explanation: 会議施設の評価は4.4で、全体満足度の4.3よりも高いです。したがって、会議施設は全体満足度より高く評価されていると推測できます。

questions.passage:
TechVista Solutions
Press Release - For Immediate Release
January 18

TechVista Solutions Announces Strategic Partnership with CloudNet Systems

SAN FRANCISCO - TechVista Solutions, a leading provider of enterprise software, today announced a strategic partnership with CloudNet Systems to deliver integrated cloud computing solutions to mid-size businesses.

Under the terms of the agreement, TechVista will integrate its flagship project management platform, TaskFlow Pro, with CloudNet's cloud infrastructure services. The combined solution will enable businesses to manage projects, store data, and collaborate in a single unified environment.

"This partnership represents a significant milestone for both companies," said Maria Gonzalez, CEO of TechVista Solutions. "Our customers have been asking for a more seamless cloud experience, and by joining forces with CloudNet, we can deliver exactly that."

The integrated platform is expected to launch in the third quarter of this year. Existing TaskFlow Pro customers will receive a complimentary three-month trial of the enhanced cloud features.

TechVista Solutions, founded in 2010, serves over 5,000 businesses in 40 countries. CloudNet Systems, established in 2015, specializes in scalable cloud infrastructure for growing businesses.

For more information, visit www.techvista.com/partnership.
questions.sentence: What is the main purpose of this press release?
choices: A) To announce a product recall
C) To announce a business partnership
D) To introduce a new CEO
questions.explanation: このプレスリリースはTechVista SolutionsとCloudNet Systemsの戦略的パートナーシップを発表するためのものです。

questions.explanation: 「The integrated platform is expected to launch in the third quarter of this year」と記載されています。第3四半期にローンチ予定です。

choices: A) A permanent discount on cloud services
B) A free three-month trial of new features
C) Priority access to customer support
D) A free upgrade to the premium plan
questions.explanation: 「Existing TaskFlow Pro customers will receive a complimentary three-month trial of the enhanced cloud features」と記載されています。既存顧客は3ヶ月の無料トライアルを受けられます。

questions.passage:
Riverside Community Center
Summer Programs Registration

The Riverside Community Center is now accepting registrations for our popular summer programs. Classes begin June 2 and run through August 22.

Yoga for Beginners
Tuesdays and Thursdays, 8:00 - 9:00 A.M.
Instructor: Amy Larson
Fee: $120 for the full session
No experience necessary. Mats provided.

Digital Photography Workshop
Saturdays, 10:00 A.M. - 12:00 P.M.
Instructor: Carlos Rivera
Fee: $180 for the full session
Participants must bring their own camera. Smartphones are acceptable.

Cooking Around the World
Wednesdays, 6:00 - 8:00 P.M.
Instructor: Chef Priya Sharma
Fee: $200 for the full session (includes all ingredients)
Explore cuisines from a different country each week.

Early registration discount: Sign up before May 15 and receive 10% off any class.
Family discount: Register two or more family members and get 15% off the second registration.

Register online at www.riversidecc.org or visit us at 45 Oak Street.
For questions, call (555) 312-7800.
questions.sentence: Which program is the least expensive?
choices: D) They all cost the same
questions.explanation: Yoga for Beginnersが$120で最も安いプログラムです。Photography は$180、Cookingは$200です。

questions.sentence: If a person registers for the Cooking class before May 15, how much will they pay?
questions.explanation: Cookingクラスの通常料金は$200です。5月15日前の早期登録割引は10%オフなので、$200 × 0.9 = $180となります。
